title: Scroll Container
description: Scroll Container renders progressively loaded lists with built-in infinite scrolling, optional node-based rows, custom templates, selection, load events, and programmatic control.
intro: Use `ui-scroll-container` for activity feeds, inboxes, logs, lists inside drawers, and any surface where users should be able to scroll through progressively loaded items without paging away.

featureSections.title: Basic infinite list
featureSections.description: The baseline pattern is a progressively loaded list of rows backed by a data source function. This is the main reason the component exists.

featureSections.title: Selectable rows
featureSections.description: Selection indicators work well for inboxes, task queues, and side lists where the current row should remain visually obvious.

featureSections.title: Custom row template
featureSections.description: Switch to a custom template when each list item needs richer metadata than a plain node row, but keep the row compact enough to preserve scan speed.

featureSections.title: Programmatic control
featureSections.description: Imperative methods such as refresh and scroll-to-edge are useful when the list responds to toolbar actions, shell state changes, or external filters.

featureSections.title: Load lifecycle events
featureSections.description: Load events help coordinate analytics, completion feedback, or outer-shell state when the container reaches the end of available data.

featureSections.title: Inbox or activity panel composition
featureSections.description: A realistic scroll container usually lives inside a denser panel with search, status, and compact actions rather than as an isolated list.

accessibility.title: Scrollable region semantics
accessibility.body: `ui-scroll-container` delegates the scrollable shell to `ui-scroll-panel` and exposes an accessible label for the scroll region through `ariaLabel`. Provide a more specific label when the default text is not enough for the surrounding screen.

accessibility.title: Keyboard behavior
accessibility.body:
Keyboard behavior depends on the rendered row content.

| Key | Action |
| --- | --- |
| Tab / Shift+Tab | Move focus into interactive rows or controls inside the scroll region. |
| Arrow keys / Page keys | Follow the browser and inner control behavior for scrolling and focused rows. |
| Enter / Space | Activate the focused row when rows are rendered as interactive `ui-node` buttons or custom controls. |

accessibility.title: Custom templates and row meaning
accessibility.body: When you replace the default node rendering with a custom template, preserve a clear row structure and accessible interactive targets. If a custom row becomes clickable, make sure that behavior remains discoverable and keyboard reachable.

apiSections.title: ScrollContainerComponent - ui-scroll-container
apiSections.lead: Standalone progressively loaded list component built on top of `ui-scroll-panel`. It can render items as `ui-node` rows or through a custom template and exposes load lifecycle events plus imperative scrolling methods.

blocks.title: Inputs
rows: Paged data source function returning a query result observable.
Number of items requested per page.
Initial page index used during first load.
Whether rows should render as `ui-node` by default.
Maps a raw item to a node row definition.
Scroll direction passed to the underlying scroll panel.
Size constraints of the scroll region.
Accessible label for the scrollable region.
Size passed to rendered node rows.
Whether selected rows show a selection indicator.
Selection indicator placement for node rows.
Renders node rows with button semantics.
Whether node rows emit selection on click.

blocks.title: Outputs
rows: Emits when a rendered node row is clicked.
Emits when a rendered node row becomes selected.
Emits after each successful page load.
Emits when the container reaches the last page.

blocks.title: Methods
rows: Scrolls the inner panel to the top.
Scrolls the inner panel to the bottom.
Clears current items and reloads from the initial page.

blocks.title: Models
rows: Paged data source contract used by the component.

paragraphs: Keep custom row templates dense and scannable. If a row turns into a complex card, users lose the benefit of smooth progressive scrolling.
Use imperative methods sparingly. Most list refreshes should still come from data changes or surrounding shell actions, not from arbitrary manual resets.
blocks.title: Notes
